sanskrit:
समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम्।
न हिनस्त्यात्मनात्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम्॥
transliteration:
samaṁ paśyan hi sarvatra samavasthitam īśvaram |
na hinasty ātmanātmānaṁ tato yāti parāṁ gatim ||
literal_meaning: One who sees the Lord equally situated everywhere does not degrade the self by the self, and thus attains the supreme goal.
contextual_meaning: Perceiving the same sacred essence in every being prevents self-sabotage and lifts consciousness to its highest potential.
psychological_state: Integrated self-acceptance replacing inner criticism with universal respect.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media, noticing envy arise, then remembering the same worth resides in every face on the feed.
anchor_text: See the same sacred essence in everyone and you won’t harm yourself.
